metrics.name: BoJ
metrics.question: Will the Bank of Japan raise its short-term policy interest rate at the June 15–16, 2026 Monetary Policy Meeting?

reversal_search.why: every actor already holds a reversal-capable state

simulation_result.question: Will the Bank of Japan raise its short-term policy interest rate at the June 15–16, 2026 Monetary Policy Meeting?
limitations: forecast decomposition — grounded prior 0.875 (counted n=3); simulation-conditional 0.0 (resolved mass 0.37); headline 0.875 via combiner_unavailable_range_only. Prior and simulation are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule.
bounded omitted-state residual 0.631: the interval widens to [0.0, 0.6314] — private-state omissions are BOUNDS, never unknown-state worlds
unresolved [unresolved_future_decision]: 0.625 — advance simulated time to the deadline; the obligation reopens the decision

no leakage-audited prior↔simulation reliability combiner is fitted — the headline is the mass-based recovery blend (resolved mass keeps its simulated conditional; unresolved mass takes the grounded prior); the two inputs are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule

blueprint.causal_thesis: The BOJ Policy Board votes on a proposal to raise the uncollateralized overnight call rate from 0.75% to 1.0%. A simple majority of the 9 members is required. The outcome hinges on whether the three April dissenters (Nakagawa, Takata, Tamura) are joined by at least two more members, or whether Gover

terminal_canonicalization.why: vote terminals read the tally directly

boj_gradual_hiking_cycle.claim: The Bank of Japan is in a gradual tightening cycle, raising rates in small increments after ending negative rates in March 2024.

provenance.definition: The Bank of Japan is in a gradual tightening cycle, raising rates in small increments after ending negative rates in March 2024.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: BOJ raised policy rate from -0.1% to 0-0.1% in March 2024, then to 0.25% in July 2024, then to 0.5% in January 2025, then to 0.75% in March 2025.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to approximately 0.75%.

dissenting_pressure_for_hike.claim: Multiple board members have consistently dissented in favor of a rate hike, indicating internal pressure for tightening.

provenance.definition: Multiple board members have consistently dissented in favor of a rate hike, indicating internal pressure for tightening.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, three members proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but were defeated 6-3.
cases.basis_quote: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but their proposals were defeated.

market_expectations_elevated.claim: Market participants and economists widely expect a rate hike at the June meeting, with Polymarket probability at 63%.

provenance.definition: Market participants and economists widely expect a rate hike at the June meeting, with Polymarket probability at 63%.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Reuters reported that the BOJ has 'locked in' signals for a June rate hike, and nearly two-thirds of economists expect 1.0% by end-June.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan has locked in signals for a June rate hike, with nearly two-thirds of economists expecting the rate to reach 1.0% by end-June.

nakagawa_junko.quantity: this member dissents on a rate hold

provenance.definition: Nakagawa Junko votes against maintaining the current rate and proposes a higher rate.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any vote where Nakagawa Junko dissents from a decision to keep the rate unchanged.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Votes where she supports a hold or is absent.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, Nakagawa Junko proposed raising the rate to 1.0% and dissented from the hold decision.
cases.basis_quote: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but their proposals were defeated.

takata_hajime.quantity: this member dissents on a rate hold

provenance.definition: Takata Hajime votes against maintaining the current rate and proposes a higher rate.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any vote where Takata Hajime dissents from a decision to keep the rate unchanged.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Votes where he supports a hold or is absent.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, Takata Hajime proposed raising the rate to 1.0% and dissented from the hold decision.
cases.basis_quote: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but their proposals were defeated.

tamura_naoki.quantity: this member dissents on a rate hold

provenance.definition: Tamura Naoki votes against maintaining the current rate and proposes a higher rate.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any vote where Tamura Naoki dissents from a decision to keep the rate unchanged.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Votes where he supports a hold or is absent.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, Tamura Naoki proposed raising the rate to 1.0% and dissented from the hold decision.
cases.basis_quote: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but their proposals were defeated.

ueda_kazuo.quantity: this governor votes with the majority on rate decisions

provenance.definition: Governor Ueda votes in favor of the final decision, whether to hold or raise.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any vote where Ueda supports the majority decision.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, Governor Ueda voted with the majority to keep the rate unchanged at 0.75%.
cases.basis_quote: The Policy Board voted 6-3 to keep the rate unchanged.

outcome_reference_class.quantity: BOJ raises short-term policy interest rate at June 15-16, 2026 meeting

provenance.definition: The BOJ announces a guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate higher than the current 0.75%.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any increase in the rate, e.g., to 1.0% or higher.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 3 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the March 18-19, 2025 meeting, the BOJ raised the rate from 0.5% to 0.75%.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to approximately 0.75%.

cases.description: At the January 23-24, 2025 meeting, the BOJ raised the rate from 0.25% to 0.5%.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to approximately 0.5%.

cases.description: At the July 30-31, 2024 meeting, the BOJ raised the rate from 0-0.1% to 0.25%.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to approximately 0.25%.

boj_policy_board.consequence_of_nonparticipation: Non-participating members do not affect the vote count; quorum must be met.

weight_invariant.rule: every weight traces to a counted reference class or is explicit unknown mass; no qualitative label is mapped to a number

reversal_search.why: every actor already holds a reversal-capable state

other_members.residual_provenance: uncovered feasible option(s) ['maintain at 0.75%', 'raise to 1.0%'] with no counted cases — bounded per option

ueda_kazuo.claim: Ueda privately believes that by June 15–16, 2026, the BOJ will have accumulated enough data (e.g., Q1 GDP, wage negotiations, inflation prints) to justify a hike to 1.0%, and he is committed to maintaining the gradual hiking cycle despite dissenting pressure.
beliefs: Gradual normalization is appropriate
Data dependence is key
Market expectations should be met to avoid volatility
goals: Maintain credibility of the hiking cycle
ueda_kazuo.pressures: Market expectations elevated; internal dissent from three members; external calls for caution from some economists.
stances: Pro-hike if data supports
Cautious on timing
distinguishing_observations: Ueda has not explicitly pre-committed to June
He often emphasizes data dependence
ueda_kazuo.action_if_state: Vote for a 25 bp hike to 1.0%, and lead the Board to a majority decision for a hike.
assumptions: Data releases between May 14 and June 15 are favorable

ueda_kazuo.claim: Ueda privately concludes that the available data by June is insufficient to confirm the sustainability of the 2% inflation target, and he prefers to wait until July or later to avoid a premature hike that could derail the recovery.
beliefs: Premature tightening risks deflationary relapse
Wage growth needs more observation
Global uncertainty is high
goals: Ensure inflation is sustainably at target
ueda_kazuo.pressures: Dissenting hawks pushing for hike; market expectations for hike; some external economists urging caution.
stances: Hold steady
Wait for more data
distinguishing_observations: Ueda has not signaled a June hike explicitly
He may prioritize caution over market expectations
ueda_kazuo.action_if_state: Vote to hold at 0.75%, and use his influence to keep the majority with him, resulting in no hike.

nakagawa_junko.claim: Nakagawa remains firmly convinced that the BOJ must raise rates to 1.0% in June to prevent the economy from overheating and to address rising inflation expectations, and she will propose a hike again.
beliefs: Inflation is above target and persistent
Accommodative policy is no longer needed
Delay risks credibility loss
commitments: Public statements on need for normalization
nakagawa_junko.pressures: Market expectations for hike; internal pressure from other hawks; external criticism of BOJ's slow pace.
stances: Hawkish
Pro-active tightening
distinguishing_observations: She has voted for hike in consecutive meetings
She often cites inflation risks
nakagawa_junko.action_if_state: Propose a 25 bp hike to 1.0% and vote in favor, attempting to sway other members.
assumptions: No new data that weakens her case

nakagawa_junko.claim: Nakagawa Junko privately believes that the global economy is entering a synchronized downturn, and that raising rates further now would trigger a severe recession in Japan, outweighing any inflation concerns. She therefore prioritizes stability over a hike.
beliefs: Global growth indicators are deteriorating rapidly
Japan's export-dependent economy is highly vulnerable
A rate hike above 0.75% would amplify recession risks
goals: Preserve BOJ credibility as a stabilizer
nakagawa_junko.pressures: Market expectations for a hike are high, but her conviction about recession risks overrides those pressures.
stances: Opposes any rate increase at this meeting
Prefers to wait for clearer global signals
historical_case_refs: BOJ's 2008 decision to hold rates during the global financial crisis
distinguishing_observations: She would vote to keep the rate at 0.75% or lower
She would argue that hiking now is premature
nakagawa_junko.action_if_state: Vote to maintain the current rate, and publicly argue for a pause citing global risks.

nakagawa_junko.claim: Nakagawa Junko privately believes that a compromise with Governor Ueda is necessary to maintain board unity and avoid a damaging split. She is willing to accept a smaller hike (e.g., to 0.85% or 0.90%) rather than her preferred 1.0%, in exchange for Ueda's support on future normalization steps.
beliefs: Board cohesion is critical for market confidence
Ueda's influence is essential for long-term policy success
A partial hike is better than no hike or a full defeat
goals: Secure Ueda's backing for future tightening
Avoid a repeat of the April 6-3 defeat
nakagawa_junko.pressures: Strong market expectations for a hike, but also pressure from Ueda to moderate her stance.
stances: Supports a moderate hike (0.85%-0.90%)
Open to negotiation on exact level
historical_case_refs: BOJ's 2000 compromise on zero interest rate policy
distinguishing_observations: She would propose a rate between 0.75% and 1.0%
She would seek a consensus statement
nakagawa_junko.action_if_state: Propose a compromise rate (e.g., 0.85%) and lobby both allies and Ueda for support.

nakagawa_junko.claim: Nakagawa Junko privately believes that inflation is about to accelerate sharply due to yen depreciation and supply shocks, and that only an immediate hike to 1.0% or higher can prevent an overshoot. She is convinced that waiting would force much larger hikes later.
beliefs: Inflation expectations are becoming unanchored
Yen weakness will import more inflation
The BOJ is behind the curve
nakagawa_junko.pressures: Market expectations for a hike are high, but she faces opposition from Ueda and gradualists.
stances: Strongly favors a hike to at least 1.0%
Would consider even higher if supported
historical_case_refs: BOJ's 1989-1990 tightening cycle to combat asset inflation
distinguishing_observations: She would insist on a hike to 1.0% or more
She would dissent if her proposal is defeated
nakagawa_junko.action_if_state: Vote for a hike to 1.0% and publicly argue that delay is dangerous.

takata_hajime.claim: Takata believes that the BOJ should raise rates to 1.0% in June to preemptively curb inflation and align with market expectations, and he will continue to advocate for a hike.
beliefs: Inflation is demand-driven
Monetary policy must be forward-looking
Low rates distort markets
commitments: Public comments on need for normalization
takata_hajime.pressures: Market expectations; internal hawkish bloc; external analysis supporting hike.
stances: Hawkish
Pro-hike
distinguishing_observations: He has been consistently hawkish
He may be influenced by yen weakness
takata_hajime.action_if_state: Vote for a 25 bp hike to 1.0% and support Nakagawa's proposal.

takata_hajime.claim: Takata believes that a rate hike above 0.75% now would trigger a global financial contagion, given fragile international markets and Japan's exposure, so he prioritizes stability over pace.
beliefs: Raising rates above 0.75% in June 2026 could spark capital flight from emerging markets, destabilizing Japan's export sector.
The BOJ's gradual hiking cycle must pause to avoid amplifying global recession risks.
goals: Prevent a systemic crisis by keeping the rate at 0.75% or lower.
Maintain Japan's financial stability as a safe haven.
takata_hajime.pressures: Market expectations for a hike are high, but Takata feels stronger pressure from global economic indicators and institutional risk management.
stances: Opposes any hike above 0.75% at this meeting.
Aligns with Ueda Kazuo on caution, despite previous disagreement on pace.
historical_case_refs: BOJ's 2008 rate cut reversal after Lehman shock
distinguishing_observations: Takata's previous vote for 1.0% was based on domestic inflation; now global risks dominate his calculus.
takata_hajime.action_if_state: Vote to keep the rate at 0.75% or lower, and argue for a wait-and-see approach in the statement.

takata_hajime.claim: Takata believes that failing to hike above 0.75% would irreparably damage the BOJ's credibility and his own reputation as a hawk, given his previous dissents for 1.0%.
beliefs: The BOJ must follow through on its hawkish signals to maintain market trust.
A hold would be seen as capitulation to dovish pressure, weakening future policy effectiveness.
goals: Establish a clear tightening trajectory to anchor inflation expectations.
Demonstrate policy independence from political influence.
takata_hajime.pressures: Intense pressure from market participants expecting a hike and from his own previous public statements.
stances: Strongly favors a hike to at least 1.0%.
Seeks to rally Nakagawa and Tamura for a unified hawkish front.
historical_case_refs: BOJ's 2000 rate hike decision to preempt inflation
Fed's 2013 taper tantrum as a credibility lesson
distinguishing_observations: Takata's prior dissents make a reversal now appear inconsistent; he feels personally committed.
takata_hajime.action_if_state: Vote for a hike to 1.0% or higher, and push for language signaling further increases.

takata_hajime.claim: Takata sees the June meeting as a strategic opportunity to shift the BOJ's internal power balance by forcing a majority vote on a higher rate, even if it fails, to position himself as the leading hawk for future leadership.
beliefs: A public dissent for a higher rate will strengthen his influence over future policy direction.
The current majority is fragile; a strong challenge could peel off undecided members.
goals: Increase his faction's sway within the Policy Board.
Signal to markets and the government that he is a credible candidate for future governor.
takata_hajime.pressures: Pressure from institutional norms to conform, but personal ambition overrides it.
stances: Proposes a rate above 1.0% (e.g., 1.25%) to differentiate from Nakagawa and Tamura.
Willing to accept defeat if it builds his long-term profile.
historical_case_refs: Fed's 1980s dissent patterns by regional presidents
distinguishing_observations: Takata's proposal exceeds his previous 1.0% stance, indicating a strategic shift rather than a pure policy preference.
takata_hajime.action_if_state: Propose a rate hike to 1.25% or more, and use the statement to highlight the need for aggressive normalization.

tamura_naoki.claim: Tamura remains committed to raising rates to 1.0% in June, viewing current policy as too accommodative given inflation above target, and will vote for a hike.
beliefs: Inflation is above target and sticky
BOJ must act to prevent overheating
Gradual pace is too slow
commitments: Statements on need for rate hikes
tamura_naoki.pressures: Market expectations; hawkish peers; external calls for action.
stances: Hawkish
Pro-hike
distinguishing_observations: He has been a consistent dissenter
He may be less swayed by short-term data
tamura_naoki.action_if_state: Vote for a 25 bp hike to 1.0% and join the hawkish bloc.

tamura_naoki.claim: Tamura believes that after three consecutive dissents for a hike to 1.0%, his credibility as a hawkish inflation fighter is on the line. He fears that if he does not secure a hike now, markets will perceive him as ineffectual, weakening his influence on future policy. He is therefore willing to acce
beliefs: Repeated dissents without success erode my reputation and the BOJ's credibility on inflation control.
A small hike now is better than no hike, as it signals progress and keeps pressure on for further moves.
goals: Restore personal credibility as a decisive hawk.
Achieve any rate increase above 0.75% to break the losing streak.
tamura_naoki.pressures: Market expectations for a hike are elevated; failing to deliver any increase could trigger yen depreciation and import price spikes, undermining the BOJ's gradual hiking cycle.
stances: Favor a hike to at least 0.85% as a face-saving compromise.
Oppose maintaining the status quo at 0.75%.
historical_case_refs: BOJ's 2000 exit from zero rates saw dissenters eventually prevail after multiple attempts; Tamura may see parallels.
distinguishing_observations: Tamura has dissented three times; a fourth failure would be unprecedented in recent BOJ history for a single member.
tamura_naoki.action_if_state: Tamura will actively lobby Nakagawa and Takata to support a compromise rate (e.g., 0.85%) and signal to Ueda that he is flexible on the exact level, but will not accept no hike.

tamura_naoki.claim: Tamura privately believes that global financial conditions have deteriorated sharply since the April meeting—due to a sudden tightening of US monetary policy or a European banking stress event—making a rate hike now dangerously premature. He fears that raising rates could trigger a credit crunch or
beliefs: The global economy is entering a risk-off phase that could spill over into Japan.
Raising rates in such an environment would be reckless and could cause a recession.
goals: Prevent a financial crisis by keeping rates unchanged.
Avoid being blamed for a policy mistake that harms the economy.
tamura_naoki.pressures: Institutional incentives to maintain majority consensus; but his shift could isolate him from former allies Nakagawa and Takata.
stances: Oppose any rate increase at this meeting.
Advocate for a wait-and-see approach until global risks subside.
historical_case_refs: BOJ's 2008 decision to hold rates during the global financial crisis, despite earlier hawkish signals.
distinguishing_observations: Tamura has not previously cited global risks in his dissents; this would be a sharp reversal.
tamura_naoki.action_if_state: Tamura will privately warn Ueda and other members about global risks, and may even vote with the majority to keep rates unchanged, breaking his dissent streak.

tamura_naoki.claim: Tamura believes that the April vote (6-3 against his proposal) showed he has maximum support from only two other members. He calculates that pushing for 1.0% again will fail, but he can use this meeting to build a case for a larger hike later by forcing a recorded dissent that highlights inflation o
beliefs: A failed dissent now is acceptable if it documents the case for faster tightening.
The majority will eventually come around as inflation persists; I must keep the pressure visible.
goals: Maintain a clear hawkish record for future policy debates.
Avoid compromising on the rate level, as any lower hike would weaken the argument for urgency.
tamura_naoki.pressures: Market expectations for a hike are elevated, but Tamura sees this as an opportunity to demonstrate that the BOJ is not moving fast enough.
stances: Propose a hike to 1.0% again, unchanged from April.
Reject any compromise below 1.0%.
historical_case_refs: BOJ's 1990-91 tightening cycle where repeated dissents by a minority eventually shifted policy.
distinguishing_observations: Tamura's proposal is identical to April; he makes no concession to the majority's concerns.
tamura_naoki.action_if_state: Tamura will submit the same 1.0% proposal, vote against any lower rate, and issue a strong statement warning of inflation risks, aiming to sway public and political opinion.

other_members.claim: The remaining three members (who voted to hold in April) have become convinced by new data or market pressures that a June hike is warranted, and they will join the hawks to form a majority for a hike.
beliefs: Data has improved enough
Market expectations are strong
Risk of inaction now outweighs risk of action
other_members.pressures: Market expectations; internal debate; external economic data.
stances: Previously cautious, now leaning hawkish
historical_case_refs: Previous shifts in BOJ voting patterns
distinguishing_observations: They are less vocal than dissenters
They may be swayed by Ueda's stance
other_members.action_if_state: Vote for a 25 bp hike, resulting in a 6-3 or 7-2 majority for hike.

other_members.claim: The other members remain cautious, believing that more time is needed to assess the economic outlook, and they will vote to hold the rate at 0.75%.
beliefs: Inflation is moderating
Wage growth is uncertain
Global risks are high
other_members.pressures: Dissenting hawks; market expectations; external advice.
stances: Cautious
Prefer to wait
distinguishing_observations: They have not publicly signaled a change
They may be influenced by Ueda
other_members.action_if_state: Vote to hold at 0.75%, maintaining the 6-3 split or shifting to 7-2 hold.

boj_policy_board.consequence_of_nonparticipation: Non-participating members do not affect the vote count; quorum must be met.

checks.note: all terminal-writing effects are known mechanical kinds
at least one resolving path exists
vote tally in members — exact units
synthetic YES→1 / NO→0 through the live path

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether wage growth will pick up and whether global risks will materialize.
deliberations.note: I weigh the tradeoff: maintaining 0.75% avoids premature tightening but risks falling behind if wage growth picks up or global risks fade. The hawks oppose, and market expectations lean toward a hike.

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether the recent economic data sufficiently supports a hike now or if waiting would better anchor inflation.
deliberations.note: The recent data on wages and consumption remains mixed, with some signs of improvement but not yet conclusive. Raising rates now could risk shocking markets and undermining credibility if the economy

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: I lack clarity on whether Nakagawa or Takata might also shift their stance, but I cannot wait for that.
deliberations.note: I am torn between my conviction that keeping rates unchanged is the prudent move to avoid a recession amid global risk-off sentiment, and the institutional pressure to maintain consensus with Nakagawa

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: The exact state of emerging market vulnerabilities and global recession indicators remains unclear, but the risk is sufficient to warra
deliberations.note: The risk of capital flight from emerging markets and global recession outweighs market expectations for a hike. Keeping rates at 0.75% aligns with my goal of preventing systemic crisis and maintaining

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether the recent wage negotiations will translate into sustained domestic demand and inflation expectations.

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether underlying inflation trends have shifted enough to justify a move.
deliberations.note: I must weigh the internal dissent from hawks like Nakagawa, Takata, and Tamura against my own cautious data-dependent stance. The lack of new decisive data since our last wait decision reinforces my b

coalescer.randomness_rule: content-addressed (state/context-seeded) draws — equal keys share the future transition law by construction; independent streams carry a tag that refuses the merge

engine_primary.weight_source: counted_reference_class_posteriors (no qualitative label is mapped to a number anywhere in this engine); represented states carry the full branch mass — omitted-state residuals are bounded interval-wideners, never unknown-state worlds

skipped_reasons: no deterministic trigger fired: primary stable, no verified evidence conflict, no reversal-capable disputed assumption (imagination alone is not a trigger)

replicate_policy.reason: behavioral replicates not requested (deterministic execution: content-addressed draws make replicate 0 exhaustive)

treatments.unresolved_future_decision: advance simulated time to the deadline; the obligation reopens the decision

provenance.quantity: BOJ raises short-term policy interest rate at June 15-16, 2026 meeting
provenance.fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 3 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

notes: no leakage-audited reliability combiner is fitted — prior and simulation are reported separately with the feasible combined range; no fixed blend is applied and Lean V2 must not become default on this basis

consequences.boundary_contract: delivery mechanics precompiled; interpretation/persuasion/reaction remain branch-local actor decisions

vote_on_rate.description: Each Policy Board member casts vote on proposed rate.
